title: Abode of Supreme Lord | BG 15.05 - 15.07 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai No views
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, the title is Abode of the Supreme Lord, dedicated to His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta
One who is free from illusion, false prestige and false association, who understands the
eternal, who is done with material lust.
How many of us are done with material lust?
So, one who is done with material lust and is free.
Freed from duality of happiness and distress.
And who knows how to surrender unto the Supreme Person, attains to the eternal kingdom.
So, in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna talks about His abode.
Unlike this material world, which is Vyakta and Avyakta.
At one point, it got created.
And Pralaya, at time, it is annihilated.
Beyond this world, which is Vyakta and Avyakta.
Which is Sanatana, which is eternal.
And we have discussed this many times.
We all have aspiration to live a life of happiness.
We all have aspiration to live a life of happiness.
We all have aspiration to live a life of peace, contentment, and we all are striving for that.
Each one of us are striving for a life.
We immediately get into how to solve that, so that I can restore back my peace, my happiness.
Then I am happy for a while.
Another something lands up in my life.
I sort the things out, hoping that now I will be happy.
Whereas the world which Krishna is talking about is the world where,
the world full of Satt, Chit and Ananda.
Permanence, Satt, Chit, full of knowledge and Ananda, bliss.
So Krishna is giving in this verse, the qualification required to go back to the world.
To that eternal abode of ours.
To that eternal abode of ours.
our eternal home. Just like for any person prison house is not the place to be. He has
got into prison house for some crime which he has committed for a reformation and ultimately
the goal is to come out of the prison house, be a law abiding citizen and enjoy a comfortable
life with all the amenities, facilities which government wants to give. So this material
world in that sense is compared to a prison house and Krishna is inviting us, we are waging
a battle, we are struggling here. There is a struggle for existence. So Krishna is inviting
us that how by developing the right attitude,
right qualities, right understanding we can go back to our original home and be eternally
happy. So what are the qualifications required? We will go one by one. The surrendering process
is described here in this particular verse. When we surrender to the Supreme Lord with
all these qualifications being the requisite.
Then we qualify to go back to that spiritual abode. The first qualification is that one
should not be deluded by pride. Pride not in the sense which we have an understanding
this world that you have a big car so I am proud of a big car. It has a much deeper connotation.
When one is freed from delusion caused by pride one can start the process of self-denial.
What is the process? When you are free from delusion caused by pride, one can start the
process of surrender. Pride stems from the understanding that I am the
controller. I am the Lord. Maybe in a small way of the things which are, that's
how we are trying to always have things in our control. Have things arranged as
per my will and wish. Frustration comes when things don't transpire the way I
want it to happen. When kids don't behave the way I want them to behave. When
things in life don't manifest the way I want it to manifest. Frustration comes. So
pride is when one thinks that I am the Lord.
And I am the enjoyer. Material life means, which keeps us away from the Lord,
where one thinks the goal is to work hard and enjoy. That's how after a long stretch
of working, people take vacation and enjoy. So that's the normal thing. Work hard and enjoy. Now

pride is due to illusion and illusion is that I am what I mentioned just now. This is illusion. You're not the maintainer. You're not the Lord. You're not the enjoyer. That's not how we are designed to be.
With pride and illusion one makes all things complicated and he is always in trouble.
Actually many times we go through messy phase in our lives, a lot of anxiety, lot of trouble.
Actually it is all our doing.
How many of you agree with this statement?
In ignorance we act and those who are wise can see that.
But those in ignorance cannot see that.
Krishna has not created this world where he feels very glad and happy that you are suffering.
He is our father. Which father would like his son to suffer?
Which father would like his son to starve to death?
Which father would like his son to die?
Which father would like his son, you know, all the kinds of anomalies what we see in the societies, tsunamis, earthquakes, natural calamities, disasters.
Which father would like his son to do?
That's why sometimes people get bewildered and say there is no God.
So he has given us a free will, choice.
And because we misuse that independence, act in ignorance, in illusion, out of pride, we mess up our lives.
So whole Bhagavad Gita, Krishna is, when he is saying surrender to me or he is asking to become his devotee, manmana, think of me.
It is not that, it is for him.
Just like, you know, somebody is there.
He gets lot of cake by somebody.
Obeying him or serving him, yes sir, or giving him respect.
He is not a lot where he is thirsty of our respect.
When he is asking us to do that, it is for our benefit.
If our life is complicated, it is because of us.
If today we are living life, it is because of us.
If today we are living life, it is because of us.
If today we are living life, it is because of us.
If today we are living a very messy life with lot of complications, lot of troubles, we are responsible.
And if Lord is too willing, the whole instruction in Bhagavad Gita is how we can, so to say, relieve ourselves from all these complications, all these entanglements, all these attachments.
All these meshes which we have created.
It creates that cocoon and it gets trapped in that very cocoon which it creates.
When one is freed from such illusion, he becomes free from all false associations caused by familial, social and national affections.
So, because of illusion, because of ignorance, we create false associations.
Now, you see here today, don't take me wrong, from one perspective I am saying, today everyone is geared up, India is playing, right?
I was expecting some dip in the attendance today, but fortunately, congratulations to all of you.
So, how many, you know, we are geared up, Indians, come on, India should win.
No doubt we should be patrioting in one sense, from one perspective, but otherwise, it's an illusion that I am Indian.
How people will be 7 hours, 8 hours, come on, India should win, India should win, isn't it?
Suppose India loses, to us, no, it shouldn't have done that, what was the hurry, why did it die?
Every time, you know, they play rationally, they should have played a little more patiently, what was the hurry?
So, here it says, false association, it's false association.
Now, somebody will be very, very sad.
At the end of the game, somebody will be very, very elated.
If you associate, some of us may not choose to watch the match today.
You are freed from that pain or elation.
We associate with the Indian team, it wins or loses, we feel joy or we feel sad.
Likewise, we have created many false associations.

We are, have associated, familial, no doubt we all are related, but if that relation is backed with the right understanding that I am eternally related to the Supreme Lord, in this life, we all have come together temporarily as brother, sister, father, mother.
I am not saying that we can go to our relatives and say,
When I joined the temple, so my parents came, you know, they were not very happy with my decision.
So, they came to convince me out of what I have chosen.
So, I went to home, you know, where they were staying with my relatives.

Then I said, son, why did you choose this?
Then I said, son, why did you choose this?
Then I said, son, why did you choose this?
Life after life we have father and mother.
Every life we get different father.
So, I heard my uncle saying,
Bula tha nah aap ko brain wash kar deta hai maa pe.

But, strictly speaking from the perspective of atma,
every life, atma has different bodies.
different body. Based on that body has different sets of relatives, different sets of associations
which are false and temporary. So what happens when one is freed from such illusion, realizes
that I am Atma, this body is a temporary dress which I am wearing. Like you might associate
with the car which you are driving, you might associate with the dress which you are wearing
but you know that you are not the dress, you know that you are not the car. Today something
happens to your dress, you change the dress and wear a new dress. Beyond a point you will
not fret over some dress which goes bad. You might feel bad but you know that you are not
the dress, you change the dress. These fake associations bind one to this material world
False understanding, illusion, one is entangled. Then he becomes freed from all dual conceptions
such as happiness and distress, pleasure and pain. When one is with knowledge, destroys
this illusion and gets freed of that pride, he comes to a state where he is equipoised.
There will be a state where he is equi-poised. There will be a state where he is equi-poised.
There will be a state where he is equi-poised. There will be a state where he is equi-poised.
There will be happiness in life, he is not overly elated and when there is distress,
he is not depressed, demoralized. He is samadarshik. He is equi-poised. He becomes full in knowledge.
Then it is possible for him to surrender. What is keeping us away from surrender, keeping
us away from the Lord, keeping us away from the knowledge because of pride, because of
